Camera The Galaxy S26 Ultra teardown reveals the use of ALoP (All Lenses on Prism) camera technology. Thats short for All Lenses on Prism, which stacks the glass horizontally instead of the traditional vertical setup. The camera lenses now have a larger diameter for better brightness, and the whole telephoto module shrank by 22% while letting in 37% more light. The aperture went from f3.4 to f2.9 on that 5x periscope shooter. Improved thermal The vapor chamber grew roughly 15%, which translates to better heat dissipation during tasks like gaming or long video recording. Whats confirmed is the layered cooling approach. There are four layers now: Advertisement Vapor chamber Graphite pad Thermal pads Newly added thermal paste Samsung even slapped

or long video recording. Whats confirmed is the layered cooling approach. There are four layers now: Advertisement Vapor chamber Graphite pad Thermal pads Newly added thermal paste Samsung even slapped thermal paste on both the storage and DRAM chips, which sit right on top of the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5. Its a dynamic approach that cements the Galaxy S26 Ultras thermal beyond legacy tricks. Battery and charging Dual connectors on the battery improve safety and efficiency by splitting the current load during fast charging. Less heat, less stress on a single connection point that shouldve been there three generations ago.
